RT by @sidhant: Consulate General of India, Toronto marked the 12th International Day of Yoga with an enthusiastic gathering of government representatives, diplomatic corps, yoga practitioners, wellness organizations, community leaders, and friends of India against the historic backdrop of the Legislative Assembly of Ontario.

Addressing the vibrant gathering, Consul General @MahavirSinghvii emphasized Yoga’s unique power to transcend cultural, linguistic, and national boundaries, bringing people together in a shared pursuit of well-being and reaffirming its timeless relevance as a universal path to health, harmony, and inner balance. Celebrating this year’s theme — 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing', a beautifully synchronized yoga session served as a powerful celebration of community, connection, and collective well-being. 

Special thanks to the yoga and wellness organizations and our valued partners whose dedication, collaboration, and outreach helped bring the message of Yoga to people across regions, generations, and walks of life.

tweets.title:
This is how Su30 fleet was created over the years : 

📅 30 Nov 1996 – 40 aircraft (Russia)
📅 18 Dec 1998 – 10 aircraft (Russia)
📅 28 Dec 2000 – 140 aircraft (HAL)
📅 10 Oct 2007 – 40 aircraft (HAL)
📅 24 Dec 2012 – 42 aircraft (HAL)

After that, there was a gap of over a decade.

A textbook example of how not to manage a procurement. OEM that forgot about upgrades, users who weren’t looking for updates (no one was bothered about upgrade) because Su57 was coming as FGFA.

tweets.title:
Many wonder why Rafale’s OSF uses two apertures while Eurofighter’s PIRATE packs most functions into a single IRST turret.

The answer lies in the era they were designed. In the 1980s and early 1990s, air forces placed significant emphasis on long-range visual identification before weapons release. Rafale’s OSF therefore combined a dedicated IRST with a separate high-resolution visual identification channel.

As sensor technology matured, modern IRSTs became capable of extracting far more information from infrared imagery itself, reducing the need for a dedicated visual-ID sensor. That’s why the more integrated PIRATE approach appears more elegant today. Initial units of typhoon did not had PIRATE, it came later.

Different design philosophies shaped by different operational requirements and not necessarily a case of one being better than the other. However OSF forces Rafale to have a smaller radar aperture.
